subject: Add server-side filtering to dmaap simulator
commit_message:
Add server-side filtering to dmaap simulator

Some of the jmeter scripts depend on DMaaP server-side filtering to
separate responses for different threads.  Added filter support to the
simulator so the jmeter scripts can be used in a local environment.
Only added the filter types that are known to be needed by the scripts.

Issue-ID: POLICY-2742
Change-Id: Ie5015cc18dc845fd8cb5a3de4912d4915464bb6f
Signed-off-by: Jim Hahn <jrh3@att.com>

Patch Set 1:

no hurry on this

Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

This is good, not sure though if the "real" DMaaP properly supports it.   I think I ran into issues in the past.

Patch Set 1: -Code-Review

> Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1
> 
> This is good, not sure though if the "real" DMaaP properly supports it.   I think I ran into issues in the past.

Good question.  If not, then maybe we shouldn't include this change.  (And I'd have to revise or deprecate feature-pooling-dmaap in drools-pdp, because it depends on it.)  I'll check with the DMaaP folks...

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1: -Code-Review
> 
> > Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1
> > 
> > This is good, not sure though if the "real" DMaaP properly supports it.   I think I ran into issues in the past.
> 
> Good question.  If not, then maybe we shouldn't include this change.  (And I'd have to revise or deprecate feature-pooling-dmaap in drools-pdp, because it depends on it.)  I'll check with the DMaaP folks...

So the answer is that ONAP DMaaP MR no longer supports server-side filtering.  Regarding this review, I can abandon it or leave it for use by some of the multi-threaded jmeter tests.  If it's abandoned, then I'll need to figure out a different mechanism that can be used in the jmeter scripts to select only the messages relevant to a given thread.  Any thoughts?

Regarding feature-pooling-dmaap, it will have to be revised to do the filtering on the client side, which means all messages will go to all drools-pdps in a given pool, unfortunately.  (Or maybe just deprecate feature-pooling-dmaap in lieu of the simpler load-balancing mechanism provided by DMaaP, itself.)  Once that's done, the filtering capability should also have be removed from policy-endpoints.

Patch Set 1:

Thought about this a little more and decided to abandon it since the jmeter scripts can't depend on server-side filtering.

Still have to address feature-pooling-dmaap.

Abandoned

server-side filtering is not supported by DMAAP MR
